source_pr.title: ddl: fix unstable restartWorker test
source_pr.body:
<!-- Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Problem Summary: 

To solve case `restart_test.go: testTableSuite.TestTableResume` failure [jenkins](https://internal.pingcap.net/idc-jenkins/blue/organizations/jenkins/tidb_ghpr_unit_test/detail/tidb_ghpr_unit_test/60599/pipeline)

Cause:

in `restartWorkers` function (only called in test), `d.ctx` is replaced by
```golang
d.ctx, d.cancel = context.WithCancel(ctx)
```

so in `doDDLJob` after we checked `d.ctx.Done()`, a replacement may happen thus `d.ctx.Err()` become `nil`, which breaks convention.

this could be tested by adding some sleep in related place, and log will be

```
["[ddl] doDDLJob will quit because context done"] [] [stack=...
```
where the empty branket means found a `nil` `d.ctx.Err()`

### What is changed and how it works?

What's Changed:

How it Works:

return `context.Canceled` instead

### Related changes

- PR to update `pingcap/docs`/`pingcap/docs-cn`:
- Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- pass original test

Side effects


### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->

- `No release note`

symptoms: Unstable restartWorkers test: `doDDLJob` observes `d.ctx.Done()` but `d.ctx.Err()` can be nil because `d.ctx` was replaced concurrently.
failure_signature: Log shows context done with missing error (nil `d.ctx.Err()`), breaking convention and causing flaky test behavior.
root_cause_explanation: The flakiness was caused by a concurrency race on DDL internal context: in tests, `restartWorkers` can replace `d.ctx` with a new `context.WithCancel(ctx)`. If `doDDLJob` selects on `<-d.ctx.Done()` while `d.ctx` is being replaced, `d.ctx.Err()` may become nil even though the done channel was observed, breaking the usual context contract and making the restart test unstable. This PR stabilizes the behavior by returning `context.Canceled` directly when the DDL context is done (instead of relying on `d.ctx.Err()`).
fix_pattern: When `<-d.ctx.Done()` fires in `doDDLJob`, return `context.Canceled` directly instead of reading `d.ctx.Err()` (which may race with context replacement in tests).

evidence: evidence: PR title: ddl: fix unstable restartWorker test
evidence: return `context.Canceled` when ddl ctx done to follow convention and prevent test flake (ddl/ddl.go)
